question:
If a parallel system consists of 3 independent components, each having 0.95 reliability, then the system reliability is given by

$$
R=1-(1-0.95)^3=0.9999
$$
answer:
If the i th component has failure time $\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{i}}$ for $\mathrm{i}=1,2, \ldots, n$ and the parallel system will fail only upon failure of all of its components, thus, the system failure time is

$$
T_P=\max _{1 \leq i \leq n} T_i
$$


Let us define $f_{\mathrm{i}}(\mathrm{t}), F_{\mathrm{i}}(\mathrm{t})$, and $R_{\mathrm{i}}(\mathrm{t})$ are, respectively, the failure time density, failure time distribution function and reliability function of the ith component. The failure time distribution function of the system is

$$
F(t)=P\left(T_P \leq t\right)=\prod_{\mathrm{i}=1}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{~F}_{\mathrm{i}}(t)
$$


The system failure time density is

$$
f(t)=\frac{d F}{d t}=\sum_{\mathrm{i}=1}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{i}}(t) \prod_{\substack{\mathrm{j}=1 \\ \mathrm{j} \neq \mathrm{i}}}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{~F}_{\mathrm{j}}(t)
$$


The system reliability is

$$
R(t)=1-F(t)=1-\prod_{i=1}^{\mathrm{n}}\left(1-\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{i}}(t)\right)
$$


The system failure rate can be obtained:
$$
r(t)=\frac{f(t)}{R(t)}=\frac{\sum_{\mathrm{i}=1}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{i}}(t) \prod_{\substack{\mathrm{j}=1 \\ \mathrm{j} \neq \mathrm{i}}}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{~F}_{\mathrm{j}}(t)}{1-\prod_{\substack{\mathrm{j}=1 \\ \mathrm{j} \neq \mathrm{i}}} \mathrm{~F}_{\mathrm{j}}(t)}
$$


If each of the $n$ independent components has an exponential failure time distribution with constant failure rate $\lambda_i$, that is $R_i(t)=e^{-\lambda_i t}$, then the system reliability is

$$
R(t)=1-\prod_{i=1}^n\left(1-e^{-\lambda_i t}\right)
$$


The mean time to failure of the system can be obtained
$$
M_{t b f}=\int_0^{\infty} R(t) d t=\int_0^{\infty}\left(1-\prod_{i=1}^n\left(1-e^{-\lambda_i t}\right)\right) d t
$$


After simplifications, we can obtain

$$
M_{t b f}=\sum_{i=1}^n \sum_{j_i=1}^n \sum_{\substack{j_{i-1}=1 \\\left\{j_1>j_2>\ldots,>j_i\right\}}}^n \ldots \sum_{j_2=1}^n \sum_{j_1=1}^n \frac{(-1)^{i+1}}{\left(\sum_{k=1}^i \lambda_{j_k}\right)}
$$


For $n=2$, from the above formula we obtain the system MTTF

$$
\begin{aligned}
M_{t b f}= & \sum_{i=1}^2 \sum_{\substack{j_2=1 \\
\left\{j_1>j_2\right\}}}^2 \sum_{j_1=1}^2 \frac{(-1)^{i+1}}{\left(\sum_{k=1}^i \lambda_{j_k}\right)} \\
= & \sum_{\substack{j_2=1 \\
\left\{j_1>j_2\right\}}}^2 \sum_{j_1=1}^2 \frac{(-1)^{1+1}}{\left(\sum_{k=1}^1 \lambda_{j_k}\right)}+\sum_{\substack{j_2=1 \\
\left\{j_1>j_2\right\}}}^2 \sum_{j_1=1}^2 \frac{(-1)^{2+1}}{\left(\sum_{k=1}^2 \lambda_{j_k}\right)} \\
= & \frac{1}{\lambda_1}+\frac{1}{\lambda_2}-\frac{1}{\lambda_1+\lambda_2}
\end{aligned}
$$
Thus,

$$
M T T F=\frac{1}{\lambda_1}+\frac{1}{\lambda_2}-\frac{1}{\lambda_1+\lambda_2} .
$$


Similarly, it can be shown that for a 3-component parallel system, the system MTTF is given by

$$
M T T F=\frac{1}{\lambda_1}+\frac{1}{\lambda_2}+\frac{1}{\lambda_3}-\frac{1}{\lambda_1+\lambda_2}-\frac{1}{\lambda_1+\lambda_3}-\frac{1}{\lambda_2+\lambda_3}+\frac{1}{\lambda_1+\lambda_2+\lambda_3}
$$
